topics.name: @wadepapenfus: Nothing better than catching giant tuna with the boys on som
details: **Signal:** 291.84x outlier (14,355,576 plays)

**Why it worked:** It's a simple, visually-driven video that taps into a universal desire for freedom, adventure, and camaraderie. The text is a simple statement of an ideal life, allowing viewers to project their own dreams onto the content.

**Your angle:** Showcase the ultimate 'Live Offline' benefit of the FieldWork program. The reel should feature Christian completely disconnected and enjoying the outdoors, visually representing the freedom that building online systems provides.

topics.name: @wadepapenfus: My 13 week old golden retriever named froth now shreds after
details: **Signal:** 276.75x outlier (13,613,170 plays)

**Why it worked:** It combines a cute, novice subject (a puppy) with an impressive, expert skill (surfing). The extreme, wholesome contrast between the subject and its capability is surprising and highly shareable.

**Your angle:** Frame a brand new AI employee or automated system as the 'puppy'. Highlight its surprising, immediate competence at running a complex part of a business, freeing the founder to get back in the field.

topics.name: @tomnoske: Earned, not given.
details: **Signal:** 37.84x outlier (619,599 plays)
**Why it worked:** It’s a short, powerful statement of values that resonates with anyone who believes in hard work. The simplicity makes it a universal anthem for personal achievement and struggle.

**Your angle:** Christian's version should flip the hustle narrative. Instead of focusing on the earning, focus on the result of smart systems: a life that was intentionally built, not just earned through brute force.

topics.name: @tomnoske: If you want to figure out your exact numbers, based on your
details: **Signal:** 13.03x outlier, FieldWork match 90 (213,437 plays)
**Why it worked:** It's a high value, zero risk offer that solves a specific, painful problem for founders. The 'comment to get it' CTA drives massive engagement, signaling virality to the algorithm.

**Your angle:** Christian should offer a free 'Automation Audit' or 'Time Freedom Calculator'. It diagnoses how many hours a founder is losing to manual tasks, directly teeing up the FieldWork promise of buying back time.

topics.name: @steveofallstreets: 2 years saved. You’re welcome
details: **Signal:** 5.07x outlier, FieldWork match 90 (21,013 plays)

**Why it worked:** Extreme brevity combined with a massive, specific promise creates a powerful curiosity gap. The confident, almost swaggering tone makes the claim feel credible and intriguing, forcing a re-watch or a comment.

**Your angle:** Frame the FieldWork promise not just as 'time saved', but as 'life reclaimed'. Quantify the hours or days returned to the user to spend offline, living their life, using Christian's grounded, confident voice.

topics.name: @takimoore: The goal isn’t to build a business that looks impressive. It
details: **Signal:** FieldWork match 100 (10,865 plays)

**Why it worked:** It validates a common founder pain point: building a business you resent. By contrasting a vanity goal (impressive) with a lifestyle goal (enjoyable), it attracts an audience that values life over hustle.
**Your angle:** Frame the business as a tool, not the end goal. The point isn't just to make money, but to use systems and automation to buy back time for the life you actually want to live.

topics.name: @takimoore: You’ve got 3 options:⁠
details: **Signal:** FieldWork match 100 (51,427 plays)

**Why it worked:** It simplifies a complex business problem into a biased A/B/C choice, validating the audience's pain while positioning the creator's method as the only logical solution. The confrontational tone and insider anecdote create authority and intrigue.

**Your angle:** Frame the three options around the core founder conflict: being trapped by your business versus building a business that frees you. Position FieldWork as the system that allows you to build online so you can live offline.

details.Hooks: Nothing better than your business running itself while you're 1,000 miles offline. | Nothing better than a Tuesday morning with no meetings on the calendar. Ever. | Nothing better than getting a Stripe notification in the middle of a national park.

details.Hooks: My 2-day-old AI employee just handled 37 customer support tickets while I was fly fishing. | This founder's lead nurture system went from zero to 11 booked calls in its first week. | The AI I built on Monday just wrote an entire week of emails better than our last copywriter.

details.Hooks: Built, not hustled. | Designed, not defaulted. | My freedom is automated.

details.Hooks: Want to know the exact number of hours your business steals from you each week? | I built a free tool that finds the one system that will buy back most of your time. | If you want to see where your business is broken, I made a free 5 minute audit.

ideas.title: State a firm, contrarian belief that pits the 'FieldWork' philosophy against the standard

details.Hooks: I firmly believe a business that makes $10k a month and takes 4 hours a week is better than one that makes $100k a month and takes 80. | I firmly believe the ROI on buying back 20 hours a week is higher than the ROI on another 20% revenue. | I firmly believe it's better to build a small business that lets you live anywhere than a big one that chains you to a desk.

details.Hooks: 20 hours a week, every week. Returned to the field. | Your next 5 years of freedom. Paid for by a system. | Build it once. Live offline for good.

ideas.title: Frame the business as a tool, not the end goal. The point isn't just to make money, but to

details.Hooks: The goal isn't to outwork everyone. It's to build a business that works without you. | The goal isn't a bigger top line. It's more time on your calendar. | The goal isn't to be online 24/7. It's to build online so you can live offline.

ideas.title: Frame the three options around the core founder conflict: being trapped by your business v

details.Hooks: You have three paths as a founder. | Your business can run one of three ways. | There are 3 ways to grow from here.

ideas.title: Frame the business as a tool to buy back your time, not an end in itself. The system isn't

details.Hooks: If you make great money but have zero time, you don't need to work harder. You need a business that works for you. | If you started a business for freedom but just built yourself a prison, you don't need more hustle. You need an escape plan. | If your business can't run for a week without you, you don't have a business. You have a high-stress job.
